name: spawn-terminal
description: Spawn a new terminal window to run CLI commands (ffmpeg, curl, python, etc.). Use for non-AI command execution.
capabilities: Spawn a new terminal window for CLI command execution
Execute development commands like npm, python, and ffmpeg
Capture and return command output when requested
Write debug logs to file for troubleshooting
Verify command syntax using --help before execution
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: This is a markdown-only documentation skill. The static scanner flagged documentation patterns (code examples in backticks, relative paths, URL references) as security issues. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES - this skill contains only documentation, no executable code, scripts, or network calls. The skill provides instructions for AI agents on how to use the fork_terminal() function for CLI command execution.
valueStatement: AI assistants cannot directly execute terminal commands. This skill enables running CLI tools like ffmpeg, curl, npm, and python in a separate terminal window for development workflows.
userTitle: Run CLI commands in a new terminal window
limitations: Cannot run AI agent spawning commands (use spawn:agent skill instead)
Requires the fork_terminal() function to be available in the environment
Output capture requires CAPTURE mode to be enabled
Limited to CLI commands, not interactive shell sessions

useCases.title: Convert video formats
useCases.description: Run ffmpeg commands to convert video files, create GIFs, or extract audio tracks.

useCases.title: Run build commands
useCases.description: Execute npm run build, webpack, or other build tools in a separate terminal window.

useCases.title: Execute Python scripts
useCases.description: Run Python scripts with proper environment setup for data processing and analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Simple ffmpeg
promptTemplates.prompt: Use spawn:terminal to run ffmpeg -i input.mp4 output.gif

promptTemplates.title: Build project
promptTemplates.prompt: Spawn a terminal and run npm run build for the current project

promptTemplates.title: Download file
promptTemplates.prompt: Use spawn:terminal to download the file using curl -O https://example.com/file.zip

promptTemplates.title: Python script
promptTemplates.prompt: Fork a terminal and run python data_analysis.py with capture enabled

bestPractices: Always verify command syntax with --help before execution
Use CAPTURE mode when you need to process command output
Use LOG_TO_FILE for debugging when commands fail
Distinguish between CLI commands and AI agent requests
antiPatterns: Skipping the --help verification step for unfamiliar commands
Assuming you know the correct syntax without checking
Using this skill to spawn AI agents (use spawn:agent instead)
Running commands without understanding what they do

faq.question: What is spawn:terminal?
faq.answer: A skill that enables AI assistants to spawn terminal windows for executing CLI commands like ffmpeg, curl, npm, and python.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Each tool must have the fork_terminal() function available.

faq.question: What commands can I run?
faq.answer: Any CLI command that runs in your terminal. Common examples include ffmpeg, curl, wget, npm, python, docker, and git commands.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides instructions to the AI. All command execution happens in your terminal. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Why not use spawn:agent for commands?
faq.answer: spawn:agent is for spawning AI assistants. Use spawn:terminal for CLI commands like ffmpeg, curl, npm, and python scripts.

faq.question: How do I get command output?
faq.answer: Set CAPTURE=true to block and return output, or use LOG_TO_FILE=true to write output to a debug file for later review.
